findings.evidence: Definitions through linearIndependent_squareClass_iff reference only Mathlib's Subgroup.square / QuotientAddGroup.zmodModule / LinearIndependent; finrank_adjoin_range (from Degree) is invoked only in the final theorem.
findings.fix: Split the generic square-class-group construction and linearIndependent_squareClass_iff into an earlier file (e.g. TauCeti/FieldTheory/SquareClassGroup.lean) importing only Mathlib, leaving only the connector finrank_adjoin_range_of_linearIndependent here over Degree.
findings.issue: Only finrank_adjoin_range_of_linearIndependent uses Degree.lean; the rest is field-generic square-class-group theory (SquareClassGroup, squareClass, ZMod 2-module instance, squareClass_prod, sum_smul_squareClass, linearIndependent_squareClass_iff, isSquare_units_val_iff) that depends on no later/multiquadratic theory and is broadly useful, but transitively imports the multiquadratic degree machinery.

summary: Imports are direct and minimal and used. But the generic square-class-group API (SquareClassGroup, squareClass, the ZMod 2 module instance, linearIndependent_squareClass_iff, isSquare_units_val_iff) depends on no multiquadratic theory and is broadly useful, yet sits in a file importing the multiquadratic Degree machinery, forcing any future consumer of the abstraction to pull in the degree tower.
